senses.definition: an indication or announcement that a situation is safe, free from danger, or no longer poses a threat

examples.x: The medical tests came back negative, giving the patient the all-clear.
After the thorough inspection, the building was given the all-clear for occupancy.
The security team issued the all-clear signal after ensuring the premises were secure.
Following the evacuation, the fire department provided the all-clear to re-enter the building.
The doctor gave the patient the all-clear, indicating the successful recovery.

senses.definition: a medical treatment in which a person is required to stay in bed for a lengthy period to recover from an illness, injury, or medical procedure

examples.x: After her surgery, the doctor prescribed two weeks of bedrest to ensure proper healing.
The patient was put on bedrest due to complications in her pregnancy.
The doctor recommended bedrest to help alleviate the symptoms of his illness.

senses.definition: recovered from a physical or mental health problem completely or compared to the past

examples.x: After using the medicine, I felt much better.
He's much better now and can return to school.
I hope you get better soon!
The doctor said she is better and can go home tomorrow.
The fresh air made her feel instantly better.

senses.definition: the act of providing treatment and paying attention to the physical and emotional needs of someone or something

examples.x: The elderly residents of the nursing home received compassionate care from the dedicated staff members.
His love for animals led him to pursue a career in veterinary care, where he could help animals in need.
The hospital provides compassionate care to all patients, ensuring their physical and emotional needs are met.

senses.definition: to provide treatment for or help a person or an animal that is sick or injured

examples.x: The nurse cares for elderly patients at the hospital.
The veterinarian will care for your dog during its recovery.
The rescue team cares for injured wildlife before releasing them back into the wild.
She stayed home to care for her sick mother.
The nurse carefully cared for the elderly patient in the hospital.

senses.definition: to regain health after an illness or become successful again after facing difficulties

examples.x: The athlete bounced back after a long injury, surprising everyone.
After a tough year, the business bounced back with higher profits.
The athlete worked hard to bounce back from a sports-related injury.
The patient's immune system helped him bounce back from the illness.

senses.definition: the process of trying to identify individuals who might have had close contact with an infected individual, particularly in order to treat or quarantine them
to gradually recover health and strength after being ill or undergoing treatment

examples.x: She used her vacation time to convalesce from a severe case of pneumonia, ensuring she returned to work fully recovered.
Patients often convalesce in a rehabilitation center where they can receive specialized care and physical therapy.

senses.definition: to clean something carefully and thoroughly to prevent the spread of infection and germs
the process of cleaning all parts of something thoroughly and with extreme care in order to prevent the spread of infection
to recover from an unpleasant or unhappy experience, particularly an illness

examples.x: It took her several weeks to get over the flu completely.
With proper treatment, most people can get over a common cold within a week.
The therapy sessions helped her get over the trauma from the accident.
After the breakup, it took him a long time to get over the pain.
She finally got over her fear of public speaking.

senses.definition: to become healthy again

examples.x: Over time, a wound may heal and gradually close.
Patients receive medical care to help injuries heal properly.
The injury is currently healing with the prescribed treatment.
Many individuals are actively healing with the help of therapy.
Patients have recently healed after undergoing medical procedures.

senses.definition: the process of becoming healthy again after an injury or illness

examples.x: The healing of her broken arm took several weeks, but she eventually regained full mobility.
Proper nutrition and rest are important factors in speeding up the healing process.
The doctor was impressed by the rapid healing of the surgical wound.
Emotional healing after a traumatic experience can take time and support from loved ones.
Physical therapy plays a crucial role in facilitating the healing of sports injuries.

senses.definition: a situation where enough people in a community are immune to a disease, making it less likely to spread
permission given by a patient to receive a particular treatment, informed of all the possible consequences and risks

examples.x: Before undergoing surgery, the patient signed a form indicating their informed consent after the surgeon explained the risks and benefits.
In medical research, participants must provide informed consent before they can join a study, understanding the procedures and potential risks involved.
The lawyer emphasized the importance of obtaining informed consent before proceeding with any legal action on behalf of the client.
The ethics committee reviewed the study protocol to ensure participants' rights were protected, including the requirement for informed consent.
Informed consent is a fundamental principle in medical ethics, ensuring patients have sufficient information to make informed decisions about their healthcare.

senses.definition: the process of boosting the immunity system of a person or animal against a disease by vaccination
to boost the immunity system of a person or animal against a disease by vaccination
(of an illness or disease) capable of being successfully healed through medical treatment or therapy

examples.x: Fortunately, her illness is curable with the right treatment and medication.
The doctor reassured the patient that his condition was curable with surgery.
Early detection is crucial for many curable diseases, as it allows for prompt treatment.
The medical team is optimistic that the new drug will make previously incurable conditions curable.
Despite the initial fear, the prognosis is hopeful, and the cancer is curable with chemotherapy.

senses.definition: a period of time spent for gradual recovery of health and strength after an illness, injury, or a medical operation

examples.x: After being discharged from the hospital, she spent her convalescence at home, gradually regaining her strength and mobility.
The doctor advised plenty of rest and a balanced diet during his period of convalescence to ensure a full recovery.
The convalescence period was challenging, but with the support of her family and friends, she slowly returned to her daily activities.
The patient’s convalescence was monitored closely by a nurse who visited regularly to check on his progress and administer necessary treatments.
His long convalescence after the accident required patience and perseverance, but he eventually regained full function of his injured leg.

senses.definition: a period during which a patient's condition improves and the symptoms seem less severe

examples.x: After several rounds of chemotherapy, the cancer went into remission, and the patient began to regain strength and energy.
The doctor was pleased to report that the patient's autoimmune disease was in remission, with no active symptoms present.
During remission, it's crucial for patients to continue regular check-ups to monitor for any signs of the condition returning.
The medication helped bring her chronic illness into remission, allowing her to enjoy a higher quality of life.
He celebrated his fifth year in remission from leukemia, grateful for the advances in treatment that made his recovery possible.

senses.definition: promoting one's health and strength after a period of injury or illness
the gradual recovery through rest after sickness or injury

examples.x: After his surgery, he spent several weeks in recuperation, gradually regaining his strength and mobility.
The doctor advised complete rest and a nutritious diet to aid in her recuperation from the severe bout of pneumonia.
Her recuperation was slower than expected, but regular physical therapy sessions helped speed up her recovery.
The patient was transferred to a rehabilitation center to continue his recuperation in a supportive environment.
Recuperation from a serious illness often requires patience and careful monitoring to ensure there are no complications.

senses.definition: the process of becoming healthy again after an injury or disease
to regain complete health after a period of sickness or injury

examples.x: After a week of rest, she started to recover from the flu.
Physical therapy helped him recover from a sports injury.
The patient is currently recovering after the surgery.
Last month, she promptly recovered from the illness.
With proper treatment, many people can recover from mental health challenges.

senses.definition: a medical test that uses special X-ray technology to create detailed pictures of the inside of a person's body
